title: Remote Work Success
description: Thrive in a remote or hybrid environment — staying productive, connected, and visible from anywhere.

modules.title: Designing Your Remote Setup
modules.description: Build a workspace and routine that maximises focus and wellbeing.

lessons.title: Creating Boundaries Between Work and Home
lessons.content:
# Creating Boundaries Between Work and Home

The biggest remote work challenge is not productivity — it is switching off.

## Physical Boundaries
- Dedicate a specific space to work, even if it is a corner of a room.
- At the end of the day, close the laptop and physically leave the space.
- Keep work equipment out of sight when not working if possible.

## Time Boundaries
- Set a consistent start and finish time and communicate it to your team.
- Use calendar blocking to make your availability visible.
- Turn off Slack and email notifications outside your working hours.

## Psychological Boundaries
- Create a start-of-day ritual (a short walk, coffee, reviewing your plan) to signal your brain that work has begun.
- Create an end-of-day ritual (a walk, writing tomorrow's to-do list, closing apps) to signal it is over.
- The commute served a psychological purpose — replace it deliberately.

modules.title: Communication and Visibility in Remote Teams
modules.description: Stay connected, build relationships, and remain visible without being in the office.

lessons.title: Over-Communicating Intentionally
lessons.content:
# Over-Communicating Intentionally

In an office, people absorb your presence and effort passively. Remote work removes that. You need to make your work visible intentionally.

## What to Communicate
- Your priorities at the start of the week (in your team channel or 1-on-1 with your manager).
- Progress updates — do not wait until the end to report.
- Blockers early — waiting until a deadline to raise an issue damages trust.
- Wins — share what you completed, not just what you are working on.

## The Friday Update
Send a brief end-of-week message to your manager:
- What I completed this week.
- What I am working on next week.
- Any blockers or risks.

This single habit significantly increases your visibility and perceived reliability.

## Choose the Right Channel
- **Async, non-urgent** → Email or a project management tool.
- **Async, team-wide** → Slack/Teams channel.
- **Complex or sensitive** → Video call.
- **Quick clarification** → Slack DM.

lessons.title: Avoiding Remote Work Isolation
lessons.content:
# Avoiding Remote Work Isolation

Loneliness is the number-one reported challenge for remote workers. Left unaddressed, it affects wellbeing and performance.

## Professional Connection
- Schedule a weekly 15-minute informal video call with a colleague (no agenda).
- Participate actively in team channels — respond to messages, celebrate wins.
- Attend virtual team social events even when optional.
- Ask your manager for regular 1-on-1s and use them to build the relationship, not just report status.

## Social Connection Outside Work
- Coworking spaces offer community 1–2 days a week.
- Professional meetups and networking events provide real-world connection.
- Online communities in your field (LinkedIn groups, Slack communities, Discord servers) connect you with peers.

## Signals That Isolation Is Becoming a Problem
- Dreading Monday more than usual.
- Decreased motivation unrelated to workload.
- Feeling invisible or undervalued despite good work.

Raise this with your manager or use employee support resources — it is a structural issue, not a personal failure.
